title: Broadsheet fold zine from A3 paper
sources: SYNTHESISED — A3 / tabloid fold-down zine structures. No pre-1928 PD source. Draw on paper-folding geometry documented in general bookbinding and paper-engineering sources.
notes: A single A3 sheet, folded and cut to produce an 8-page A6 zine — the classic one-sheet one-cut structure scaled up. The A3 version produces a slightly larger page (A6 size) than the A4 version, and the thicker page weight reads more substantial. Covers: the fold sequence (fold to A4, fold to A5, fold to A6), the single cut (through the centre pages only, unlocking the folding sequence), opening the whole sheet and re-folding to form the book block. The result is 8 pages (front cover, 6 interior pages, back cover) all from one sheet with no adhesive. Distinguish from: the eight-page A4 mini-zine (which uses a smaller sheet for a smaller page), the saddle-stapled zine (which uses multiple sheets).
